title: The Mir Yeshiva's Miraculous Journey Through Shanghai
title_zh: 米尔神学院的上海奇迹之旅
title_he: המסע המופלא של ישיבת מיר דרך שנחאי
summary: The remarkable story of how approximately 300 Torah scholars from Poland's Mir Yeshiva escaped Nazi persecution and spent five years (1941-1946) studying in Shanghai, China - the only place in the world that required no visa for entry. Despite extreme poverty, disease, and hardship in the Shanghai Ghetto, the yeshiva maintained its full study schedule, preserving a thousand-year tradition of Lithuanian Jewish scholarship. Today, the Mir Yeshiva thrives with thousands of students in Jerusalem and Brooklyn.

historical_periods.chinese_name: 抗日战争 / 中华民国时期

cultural_parallels: Reverence for classical scholarship and learning
Preservation of ancient texts during times of upheaval
Scholar as cultural hero in both traditions
Mutual respect between Chinese and Jewish communities in Shanghai
